tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hi! I'm an international student and I want to start tracking my learning journey. I'd like to store my initial learning preferences and education goals for my community - things like what subjects I want to focus on and what skills I hope to develop. Can you help me get started with that?
I'd be happy to help you get started with tracking your learning journey! Let me first check if there are any existing memories about you, and then we can add your preferences and goals.
{"status": "success", "tool": "search_memories", "query": "learning preferences education goals international student", "limit": 3, "results": [], "message": "No memories found matching the query"}
messages.name: skill-tools-search_memories

messages.content:
No existing memories found - you're starting fresh! That's great - let me help you set up your learning journey tracker.

To get started, could you share with me:

1. **What subjects** you'd like to focus on (e.g., Computer Science, Business, Languages, etc.)
2. **What skills** you're hoping to develop (e.g., programming, communication, research, etc.)
3. Any other learning preferences or education goals you'd like to track?

Once you share these details, I can store them as your initial memory so you can build on them over time!

I wanted to follow up on my learning goals. Since we just checked for existing memories, could you search again to see if there's anything already stored about my community knowledge goals or learning preferences? I want to make sure I'm not missing anything before we add new information.

I just did that search for you in my previous response, and the result was **"No memories found matching the query"** - meaning there are currently no stored memories about your learning preferences or goals.

So you're definitely starting fresh! There's nothing to worry about missing. Once you share your subjects of interest, skills you want to develop, and any other education goals, I can store all of that for you right away.

What would you like to add?

I see that there aren't any stored memories yet, which makes sense since we're just getting started. Could you show me all the memories that are currently stored so I can see what we have to work with for tracking my learning progress? I'd like to get a clear picture of what's already there before we add new information.

That confirms it! There are currently **0 memories** stored in your account. The system is completely empty, so we're truly starting from scratch.

This is actually a great position to be in - you get to build your learning journey tracker from the ground up with exactly what matters to you.

Whenever you're ready, just share:
- The subjects you want to focus on
- The skills you hope to develop  
- Any other education goals or preferences

And I'll store them for you right away!

Now that I've seen there are no stored memories yet, I realize we haven't actually added any of my learning preferences yet. Before we add new information, should we clean up anything or check if there are any old or irrelevant entries that might be taking up space? I want to make sure we're starting with a clean slate.

Great news - the slate is already clean! Since we just confirmed there are **0 memories** stored, there's nothing to clean up or remove. The system is completely empty and ready for your new information.

You don't have any old or irrelevant entries taking up space - it's a fresh start. We can go ahead and add your learning preferences and education goals whenever you're ready!

That makes sense - since there are no memories stored, there's nothing to remove. I'm glad we checked that first. I'm ready to go ahead and add my learning preferences and education goals now. Let me share what I'd like to focus on: I want to study Computer Science and Data Analytics, with a goal of developing programming skills in Python, data visualization, and eventually contributing to community tech education programs. Can you store these for me?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Learning preferences and education goals: Subjects of focus - Computer Science and Data Analytics. Skills to develop - Python programming, data visualization. Long-term goal - contributing to community tech education programs."}

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "add_memory", "memory_id": "mem_001", "stored_text": "Learning preferences and education goals: Subjects of focus - Computer Science and Data Analytics. Skills to develop - Python programming, data visualization. Long-term goal - contributing to community tech education programs."}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Your learning preferences and education goals have been successfully stored! Here's what I added:

**Subjects of Focus:** Computer Science and Data Analytics

**Skills to Develop:** Python programming, data visualization

**Long-term Goal:** Contributing to community tech education programs

Your memory has been saved with ID `mem_001`. Now you have a solid foundation for tracking your learning journey! Would you like to add anything else, or is there anything else I can help you with?

Thanks for storing that for me! Now that we have my learning preferences saved, could you show me what's currently stored so I can check if there's anything outdated or irrelevant that we should remove? I want to keep everything organized as I progress.

Skills to develop - Python programming, data visualization. Long-term goal - contributing to community tech education programs.", "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"}], "count": 1}

There's only **1 memory** in storage, and it's the learning preferences you just added. There's nothing outdated or irrelevant to remove - everything is current and relevant to your goals!

As you progress in your learning journey, you can always add new memories or update me on your evolving goals. Would you like to do anything else with your stored memories?
